root_text.an3.83:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, kassako gahapati paṭikacceva khettaṁ sukaṭṭhaṁ karoti sumatikataṁ.
root_text.an3.83:1.4: Paṭikacceva khettaṁ sukaṭṭhaṁ karitvā sumatikataṁ kālena bījāni patiṭṭhāpeti.
root_text.an3.83:1.5: Kālena bījāni patiṭṭhāpetvā samayena udakaṁ abhinetipi apanetipi.
root_text.an3.83:1.6: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi kassakassa gahapatissa pubbe karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.83:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tīṇimāni bhikkhussa pubbe karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.83:2.4: imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi bhikkhussa pubbe karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.83:3.2: ‘tibbo no chando bhavissati adhisīlasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo no chando bhavissati adhicittasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo no chando bhavissati adhipaññāsikkhāsamādāne’ti.

translation_text.an3.83:1.1: “Mendicants, a farmer has three primary duties.
translation_text.an3.83:1.3: A farmer first of all makes sure the field is well ploughed and tilled.
translation_text.an3.83:1.4: Next they plant seeds in season.
translation_text.an3.83:1.5: When the time is right, they irrigate the field and then drain it.
translation_text.an3.83:1.6: These are the three primary duties of a farmer.
translation_text.an3.83:2.1: In the same way, a mendicant has three primary duties.
translation_text.an3.83:2.3: Undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.
translation_text.an3.83:2.4: These are the three primary duties of a mendicant.
translation_text.an3.83:3.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.83:3.2: ‘We will have keen enthusiasm for undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.’
